page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on external storage options, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the AMD EPYC processor delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3115.38, placing it in the top 10% of tested hardware. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12465.68. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, recording 614.87 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, 47.95 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, and 28.19 GB/sec for uncached large-block writes. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 29.54 GB/sec, while bzip3 decompression scales from 13.4 GB/sec on a single thread to 53.2 GB/sec in multi-threaded operations.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate vector calculations and machine learning inference tasks, though it lacks AMX support. Cryptographic operations are aided by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and strong single-core compute capabilities, this instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as web serving, batch processing, and single-threaded compilation tasks, offering a balanced cost-to-performance profile for compute-bound applications without heavy memory requirements.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or GPU resources. The instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, scoring 3115.38 in stress-ng tests, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. It supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and AES-NI. With a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as web serving, data compression, and batch processing that benefit from high single-core efficiency without requiring massive memory footprints.

tagline: An 8-vCPU compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and top-tier single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
